seo.title: Building Data Extraction with Smolagents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build data extraction with Smolagents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Smolagents simplifies building lightweight agents for tasks like data extraction from unstructured documents using schema-guided LLM reasoning. However, its code-based approach exposes risks like unsafe code generation and schema non-compliance that can lead to inaccurate or incomplete extractions. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure schema compliance, field accuracy, and type consistency, making your agents production-ready.

architectureOverview.heading: Data Extraction Architecture with Smolagents
architectureOverview.description:
Smolagents agents typically consist of a code agent that generates Python functions for parsing unstructured text and a tool agent that calls LLM-powered extraction tools guided by JSON schemas. The workflow starts with document ingestion, followed by schema-defined extraction where the agent reasons step-by-step to populate fields like names, dates, and amounts. Multi-step reasoning handles complex documents by breaking extraction into sequential tool calls, but lacks built-in validation.

The lightweight design emphasizes minimal dependencies, using Hugging Face models for reasoning and Pydantic-like schemas for structured output. Agents execute generated code directly, making verification critical for production use cases like invoice processing or contract analysis. Vex wraps the agent.run() call to validate outputs against predefined checks before returning results.

risks.name: Unsafe Python Execution
risks.description: Code agent generates malicious or infinite-loop code during extraction
risks.mitigation: Wrap agent.run() with Vex sandboxed execution

risks.name: Schema Non-Compliance
risks.description: Extracted fields missing required properties or wrong types
risks.mitigation: Vex schema compliance checks with Pydantic validation

risks.name: Field Accuracy Errors
risks.description: LLM hallucinates values not present in source document
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks against source text

risks.name: Incomplete Extractions
risks.description: Multi-step reasoning drops fields in long documents
risks.mitigation: Vex completeness validation ensuring all schema fields populated

implementation.title: Define Extraction Schema
implementation.description: Create Pydantic model defining expected output structure for invoice extraction
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional, List

class InvoiceLineItem(BaseModel):
    description: str = Field(..., min_length=1)
    quantity: float
    unit_price: float
    total: float

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    invoice_number: str
    date: str
    customer_name: str
    line_items: List[InvoiceLineItem]
    subtotal: float
    tax: Optional[float] = 0.0
    total: float

implementation.title: Create Extraction Tool
implementation.description: Define Smolagents tool that uses LLM to extract structured data

implementation.title: Build Extraction Agent
implementation.description: Configure Smolagents with extraction tools and schema guidance
implementation.code:
from smolagents import Agent
from pydantic import BaseModel

class Invoice(BaseModel):
    # ... schema from step 1

agent = Agent(
    tools=[extract_invoice],
    llm=llm,
    instructions="""You extract invoice data from documents.
    Always return complete, valid JSON matching the schema.
    Never hallucinate values not in the source text."""
)

implementation.title: Basic Agent Execution
implementation.description: Run agent without verification (shows why Vex is needed)
implementation.code:
invoice_text = """
Invoice #12345
Date: 2024-10-15
Customer: Acme Corp

Item: Widget A, Qty: 2, $50.00 = $100.00
Item: Widget B, Qty: 1, $75.00 = $75.00

Subtotal: $175.00
Tax: $14.00
Total: $189.00
"""

result = agent.run(f"Extract this invoice: {invoice_text}")
print(result)
